title: Domain Evidence, Regulation, Standards, and Licensing Boundaries · Claim Repair Board
summary: A preserved decision record for domain evidence, regulation, standards, and licensing boundaries contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in domain authority and compliance boundary dossier and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.
Record version, status, jurisdiction, applicability, review date, supersession, and monitoring owner for governing sources.
Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Authority Hierarchy
steps.instruction:
debug case — Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.

Inspect the Authority Hierarchy trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Authority Hierarchy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Authority Hierarchy, keep Authority Hierarchy connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Authority Hierarchy, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Authority Hierarchy, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Authority Hierarchy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Authority Hierarchy, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Authority Hierarchy, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: All official-looking web pages impose equivalent requirements.

stimulus.title: Authority Hierarchy case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: All official-looking web pages impose equivalent requirements.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority. Artifact under review: domain authority and compliance boundary dossier.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Authority Hierarchy, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Authority Hierarchy record says domain authority and compliance boundary dossier looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Authority Hierarchy.

For inspect the failure record, apply Authority Hierarchy without its stated preconditions, omit the domain authority and compliance boundary dossier trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Authority Hierarchy for domain authority and compliance boundary dossier.

content.code:

  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Compliance Nonclaim structural check is only one gate. domain authority and compliance boundary dossier must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Compliance Nonclaim

Write the debug evidence note for Compliance Nonclaim: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions.
